Sub-problem 1:  
The director has planned scenes inspired by 5 different iconic Akshay Kumar movies, each requiring a different number of shooting days: 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 days respectively. To optimize the schedule, the director wants to shoot these scenes in such a way that the total number of shooting days is minimized while ensuring that no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade. The decades are {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, and {2010-2019}. The release years of the movies are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015. An optimal schedule must ensure that no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade.  

The optimal schedule is:  
- Scene 1 (1994): 4 days (from 1990-1999)  
- Scene 2 (2004): 6 days (from 2000-2009)  
- Scene 3 (2007): 8 days (from 2000-2009)  
- Scene 4 (2012): 10 days (from 2010-2019)  
- Scene 5 (2015): 12 days (from 2010-2019)  

This schedule ensures that no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade, and the total number of shooting days is minimized.  

******  

Sub-problem 2:

The cost per day for shooting each scene is proportional to the complexity of the scene and the iconic status of the Akshay Kumar movie it represents.
